name: Input
description: Bare text-field surface. Variant maps to HTML <input type='…'>. Apollo v2 wraps it in higher-level Field/Field Group molecules for label + helper + error composition; this PoC covers the atom only.
$matrixNote: Smallest atom-batch matrix so far: 15 cells (Button 264 → Switch 64 → Input 15). All cells uniform 373w × 44h. No size/type/placement axes. WCAG 2.5.5 compliant baseline.

$missingStates.$comment: Same Hover-absence pattern as Switch (F5). Pressed not semantically applicable to inputs. Loading not applicable (Inputs don't have async-load states; Field molecule could).

$newStateValue.name: Filled
$newStateValue.$comment: NEW state value not seen in Button or Switch matrices. Represents 'user has entered text'. Implementation: text-color shifts placeholder muted-foreground → base/foreground; typography stays Regular weight (no font-weight change). Distinguishes empty placeholder rendering from typed-content rendering at the component level.

exposedProps.name: placeholderText
exposedProps.description: Placeholder text shown when input is empty (or typed-text on Filled state).

slots.name: placeholder
slots.$role: The placeholder OR the typed text. Apollo v2 source DS doesn't distinguish — Filled state changes the styling but the slot is the same TEXT node. Renderer maps to either <input placeholder> or <input value> as appropriate.
slots.$typographyNote: Regular weight across ALL states including Filled. Step-1 inferred medium-weight on Filled was wrong; confirmed via cell walk.

fill.$filledNote: Filled state shifts placeholder text-color from muted-foreground → foreground. THE distinguishing visual change for typed content.
fill.$confirmed: Walked Default, Focus, Disabled, Filled, Invalid cells.

slots.name: fileButton
slots.$role: ONLY present on variant=File. Pseudo-button 'Choose file' affordance.
slots.$typographyNote: File button uses MEDIUM weight (distinguishes from filename text below which is normal weight).
slots.$confirmed: Walked cell 73:43 (File/Default). Same slot reappears across all 5 File-variant state cells.

slots.name: filename
slots.$role: ONLY present on variant=File. Shows 'No file chosen' or selected filename. Takes remaining horizontal space (flex-1).
slots.$confirmed: Walked cell 73:43. Apollo v2 emits 'No file chosen' as the default text — same as native browser file inputs.

gap.$comment: gap=spacing/1 (4px) across all variants. Only File variant has 2 visible children (button + filename) so gap is actually visible; Default/Password have single child.

container.$paddingNote: Asymmetric padding: 4 vertical + 10 horizontal. Captured per SP-7 per-side.
container.$widthNote: Container width 373px hardcoded across all 15 cells. Same audit class as Button/Switch container widths.

height.$comment: 44px across all 15 cells. WCAG 2.5.5 compliant baseline.

radius.$comment: 10px across all cells. Inputs are rounded-rectangles, NOT pills. Different identity from Button (pill 9999) and Switch.track (pill 9999).

container-fill.$invalidFillNote: F13 (NEW, LOW): Invalid bg emits as CSS linear-gradient with identical stops (both #fafafa). Effectively a solid fill. Likely Figma export oddity OR placeholder for future visual treatment (e.g. subtle red tint). Source-DS audit candidate.
container-fill.$disabledNote: Disabled state uses input-50-dark-input-80 (the @50% alpha input border color, hex #e5e5e580) AS THE BG. Combined with opacity-50 on the outer container, the visual effect is a doubly-muted background.

color.$focusNote: Focus swaps the border from input gray (#a3a3a3) → ring violet (#5a35c0).
color.$confirmed: Walked all 5 Default-variant state cells. Disabled border STAYS at base/input (not muted-foreground); the diminished look comes from the opacity-50 container.

container-shadow.$invalidNote: Same pattern as Switch F11 — Invalid state shows destructive ring AT REST, not only on Focus. Apollo v2 intentional emphasis pattern across atoms.

container-overflow.$comment: Focus + Invalid emit overflow-clip; others have no overflow set (visible default). Same pattern as Switch.

opacity.$disabledNote: F8 reconfirmed: Input uses opacity-50 for Disabled — same as Switch. CONSISTENT WITHIN ATOMS (Switch + Input both use opacity-50), but still inconsistent with Button (which uses tinted color, no alpha).

Default.$visualIdentity: Single placeholder line. State drives all visual changes.

Password.$visualIdentity: IDENTICAL to Default at the Figma level. Apollo v2 doesn't show masked dots in source — that's a browser/renderer concern. The variant axis exists purely for codeConnect/HTML-type mapping.
Password.$auditNote: Could surface Apollo v2 question: should Password show a 'show/hide eye toggle' affordance? Currently no.

File.$visualIdentity: Two children: 'Choose file' pseudo-button (font-medium) + filename slot (font-normal, flex-1).

touchTargetCompliance.$comment: First atom in PoC with 100% touch-target compliance. All 15 cells = 44h.

verificationFailures.auditFinding: F12 (NEW): Apollo v2 Input cells default their Figma documentationLink to '#with-label' fragment, but the bare Input atom doesn't render a label — that's the Field molecule. Only Disabled (#disabled) and File (#file) variants have correct fragments. Same audit class as Button.Link's #ghost → #link typo (F3).

$step1_token_findings: 24 tokens used. Smallest delta of atom batch: 22 shared with Button+Switch, only 2 NEW.
NEW: base/input (#a3a3a3) — first atom to elevate the input border color to a top-level semantic token.
NEW: custom/input-50-dark-input-80 (#e5e5e580) — used as the Disabled state BG fill. Combines with opacity-50 outer-container for a doubly-muted look.

$step2_known_gaps.field: Password variant has no eye/show-toggle affordance
$step2_known_gaps.resolution: Confirmed as DS design choice — Password is semantically distinct from Default but visually identical. Source-DS audit: consider adding show/hide toggle in v2.next.

$step2_known_gaps.field: File variant filename text '#No file chosen' is hardcoded text (not exposedProp)
$step2_known_gaps.resolution: OPEN — could become a TEXT exposedProp on File variant only (via SP-1 whenVariant filter). Currently not exposed.

$step2_known_gaps.field: Invalid bg gradient with identical stops
$step2_known_gaps.resolution: DOCUMENTED — F13 (NEW, LOW). Figma export oddity OR intentional placeholder. Source-DS audit candidate.

$step2_known_gaps.field: Container width 373px hardcoded raw, not bound
$step2_known_gaps.resolution: Confirmed hardcoded across all walked cells. Same class as Button/Switch container widths.

$new_audit_findings_from_step3.F12: NEW (LOW). Input cells default Figma documentationLink to '#with-label' fragment. Should be base URL (no fragment) or component-specific. Affects 13/15 cells. Same class as Button.Link F3 (#ghost → #link).
$new_audit_findings_from_step3.F13: NEW (LOW). Input Invalid state emits container-fill as CSS linear-gradient from-#fafafa to-#fafafa (identical stops). Figma export oddity or intentional placeholder.

v0.2.0: SP-1 — whenVariant filters exercised on slots[fileButton] and slots[filename] (only present on variant=File)
SP-2 — variantConstraints empty (no axis combinations forbidden)
SP-3 — n/a (no textDecoration usage in Input)
SP-4 — $bindingStatus: hardcoded fires on container width (373px)
SP-5 — n/a (no slot swaps on state transitions)
SP-7 — Per-side asymmetric padding (4px vertical, 10px horizontal) captured. clipsContent emitted per cell.
SP-8 — n/a (no nested INSTANCEs in Input atom — Field molecule will exercise this)
v0.3.0: SP-4 extension — semantically-mismatched: NOT observed in Input (no cross-axis token usage)
SP-9 — Not applicable. Input has no animation. Confirms spec doesn't force animation declarations where none exist.
